steps.title: Identify opposite and hypotenuse
steps.content: The figure shows a right triangle with hypotenuse $14$ and an acute angle of $58^\circ$. Segment $AB$ is opposite the $58^\circ$ angle.

steps.title: Use the sine ratio
steps.content: Sine relates opposite side to hypotenuse: $$\sin \theta=\frac{\text{opposite}}{\text{hypotenuse}}.$$ Here that gives $$\sin 58^\circ=\frac{AB}{14}.$$

steps.title: Solve for AB
steps.content: Multiply both sides by $14$: $$AB=14sin 58^circ.$$ This is an expression choice question, so a decimal value is not needed.

steps.title: Check the choices
steps.content: Choice A is $14\sin 58^\circ$, which matches the expression for the opposite side. Choice C would use cosine and gives the adjacent side instead. Choices B and D divide by a trig ratio, which would be used if $14$ were a leg rather than the hypotenuse. Choice A is correct.
